choiceresponse.label:
A patient has the following active indicators:
cp_2 = 1
thal_2 = 1
sex_0 = 1
exang_0 = 1 Which statements are true? (Select all that apply.)

checkboxgroup.choice: Each of these indicators contributes positively to the model’s predicted risk.
The combined predicted risk is influenced by the sum of their coefficients.
Removing one of these indicators would lower the model’s predicted risk.
These indicators guarantee that the patient has heart disease.
The model uses these indicators independently and additively.

div.p: All listed indicators have positive coefficients, so each pushes the prediction upward. Logistic regression combines these effects additively, meaning the total predicted risk reflects their sum. Turning any one of these from 1 to 0 removes its positive coefficient from the sum, decreasing the log‑odds and therefore the predicted probability. This does not guarantee an outcome; it only reflects how the model aggregates information.
